notes.resolve: Lite & Verified resolve % are REAL, from swe-bench/experiments (results/results.json, resolved/denom). Pro % are from the Scale SEAL public standardized set (morphllm.com/swe-bench-pro). Darwin entries are our own gold-graded runs on the official swebench harness. LiveCodeBench pass@1 is REAL, scored by the official lcb_runner codegen_metrics harness (private tests, never seen during generation).
notes.cost: Official entries' cost/instance is ESTIMATED from each system's DISCLOSED model(s) + scaffold shape × public token pricing — not a figure those teams published; order-of-magnitude only (marked 'est'). 'undisclosed' = no public model/cost. Darwin costs are MEASURED from actual OpenRouter spend.
notes.darwin: Darwin is measured on SWE-bench Lite (conformant: solver never sees gold tests in-loop). Verified/Pro runs are not done yet — those boards are shown for competitive context.
notes.livecodebench: Darwin LCB point: single-shot deepseek-chat on a balanced n=25 SUBSET of release_v5's >=2024-12-01 window (post-cutoff, contamination-resistant). Scored by the OFFICIAL lcb_runner harness (eval validated end-to-end: known-correct→PASS, empty/wrong→FAIL). n=25 is DIRECTIONAL (wide CI) and skews easy/medium vs the full release_v5 average — NOT directly comparable to the leaderboard's whole-set numbers. Official reference points shown are whole-release_v5 (or vendor-disclosed window) for context only.

draco.label: DRACO — Deep Research (quality)
draco.metricNote: Different metric: 'Resolve %' here = DRACO research-quality × 100 (0–100), NOT bug-resolve. 'Cost' is per full corpus run (MEASURED, not estimated); Run total = that run cost.
draco.darwinNote: DRACO is our own cross-domain research benchmark. The cheap model beats the frontier on BOTH quality and cost — the harness/cost-router is the lever. All numbers measured (qualityPerUSD).

livecodebench.label: LiveCodeBench (n=25, release_v5 ≥2024-12)

entries.name: Darwin · single-shot
entries.note: 16/25 (easy 8/9, med 6/8, hard 2/8). OFFICIAL lcb_runner harness; eval validated (correct→PASS, empty/wrong→FAIL). Contamination-resistant window (post-cutoff). n=25 DIRECTIONAL; skews easy/med vs full-set — not 1:1 with leaderboard.

entries.name: Darwin · +TDR repair (public-test)
entries.note: 16/25 — NO net lift. Public-sample-only feedback overfits (abc384_g PASS→FAIL after repair; one unrelated +1 was temp-0 nondeterminism). Passing the visible sample ≠ passing hidden tests on cheap candidates.

entries.name: DeepSeek-V3 (official, full release_v5)
entries.note: Vendor-disclosed LCB pass@1 (08.01–12.01 window, whole set) — full-set average over harder/older problems; NOT 1:1 with our balanced n=25 sample.

livecodebench.darwinNote: Single-shot deepseek-chat on a balanced n=25 SUBSET of release_v5's ≥2024-12-01 (post-cutoff) window. Scored by the OFFICIAL lcb_runner codegen_metrics harness; eval validated end-to-end before trusting the number. n=25 is directional (Wilson CI [44.5,79.8]) and easy/med-skewed — the official whole-set reference (~34%) is a different, harder population, shown for context only. TDR-style public-test repair gave NO lift here (overfits the visible sample).
